action.reasoning: Observation: The checkpoint inspection reveals the key mismatch: best checkpoint meta says esm_input_dim=960, while cached t12 features are 1440-dim and my recent inference path used the wrong ESM recipe, causing the severe score drop. Analysis: The most plausible training-time ESM feature was a 960-dim concatenation of full-sequence mean + hotspot mean from ESM2-t6 (640+320? no) or more likely t12 with a specific projection/slice. Since the checkpoint already scored 0.7242, highest ROI is to reconstruct the exact 960-dim feature recipe empirically from training artifacts. We have train/val labels and cached 1440-dim t12 features, so I can test candidate transforms against the saved ESM head on val and compare Spearman. Once the correct transform is identified, patch predict.py to use it and resubmit the known-best checkpoint. Decision: write a small diagnostic script to score candidate 960-dim transforms of cached t12 features with the saved ESM head on val, then update predict.py accordingly and submit the best checkpoint.
